ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello for Thomas Lucas (Trustee).

The Court questions whether the Special Needs Trust and all of the expenses built into it are benefitting the Beneficiary. Ms. Robello stated that circumstances have changed with the Beneficiary, that the expenses are minimal, that they have to do with repairs to the wheelchair and a cell phone, and that at this point these are his needs. Ms. Robello also stated that the Beneficiary now lives with family, that he had previously lived on his own, and that they still have to do accountings and administration. In addition, Ms. Robello stated that the Beneficiary has no other needs, that she can speak with the Trustee to see if there is anything else the Beneficiary needs, that they are in a period of stability and that she can discuss a spend down with the Trustee. Furthermore, Ms. Robello stated that she did a supplement (copy provided) and that she submitted time sheets to support the Trustee's fees. The Sixth Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-13-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheila Robello for Thomas Lucas (Trustee).

Ms. Robello addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes with regard to expenditures for hotel stays, televisions, rents, eye glasses, moving, etc. Ms. Robello also explained circumstances as well as Mr. Burns medical condition and stated that she believes bond was porperly reduced, that it was her mistake to request again and that she will resolve the issue. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On Ex Parte Petition For Order Authorizing And Directing Sale Of Trust Automobile To Family Member
Off Calendar Apr-18-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Sheila Robello appearing for and with trustee Thomas Lucas. Ernest Der appearing as court appointed counsel for Tommie Burns. Mr. Burns wishes to give the car to his brother and not sell it to Ms. Ginn. Court wants to honor Mr. Burns' wish. The car is to be a gift to Spencer Burns. Title will be transfer to Spencer Burns. Ms. Robello to prepare an order.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: F. Smith.
